grades.expectation: Response identifies 68 years as a critical lease length issue
grades.reason: Explicitly states 68 years is in the 'CRITICAL' band for saleability and mortgageability

grades.expectation: Response mentions specific lender names and their minimum requirements (not just 'most lenders want 70+ years')
grades.reason: Lists specific lenders with exact requirements: Halifax, Barclays, Lloyds, TSB (70 years), Nationwide (55 years), Santander, Virgin Money (85+ years)

grades.expectation: Response mentions marriage value applies below 80 years
grades.reason: Clearly states 'marriage value applies under current law' because lease is below 80 years

grades.expectation: Response discusses lease extension options (statutory Section 42, informal, assign benefit)
grades.reason: Covers all three options: statutory Section 42 notice, informal/voluntary deal, and assigning benefit of Section 42 claim

grades.expectation: Response quantifies the price impact (discount percentage or range)
grades.reason: States 'expect a meaningful discount vs comparable long leases—often 15–30%+' in this territory

grades.expectation: Response mentions reduced buyer pool due to mortgage restrictions
grades.reason: Explicitly states 'buyer pool shrinks sharply' and 'you'll lose many first-time buyers and anyone reliant on mainstream lenders'
